content.text:
```mdl
@ Confidentiality (Non‑Disclosure) Agreement – Model for NDA #12‑17
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_12_nda_17

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Parties to the Agreement
type Party = 
    | Broker(unit)      # BROOKS’ BOTTLING COMPANY, LLC
    | Client(unit)      # CLIENT (the other party)

@ Information that is subject to the confidentiality obligations
type ConfidentialInfo = {
    owner: Party,                     # who originally owns the information (Client)
    content: string,                  # free‑form description (not used in logic)
    received_by_brooks: bool,         # true when BROOKS has obtained the info
    # Exception flags (see Section 3)
    already_possessed: bool,           # 3.1 – BROOKS already possessed it
    developed_independently: bool,    # 3.2 – BROOKS developed it independently
    rightfully_received: bool,        # 3.3 – received from a third party without duty
    inadvertent_disclosure: bool,     # 3.4 – disclosed inadvertently
    client_consent: bool,             # 3.5 – CLIENT gave prior written consent
    service_disclosure: bool,         # 3.6 – disclosure necessary to perform services
    publicly_available: bool          # disclosed by law or public domain
}

@ A copy of confidential information that may be created by the receiving party
type ConfidentialCopy = {
    original: ConfidentialInfo,
    created_by: Party,
    created_at: string                # timestamp placeholder (not used in logic)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ The two parties to the NDA
entity broker: Party      @ BROOKS
entity client: Party      @ CLIENT

@ The set of confidential items exchanged under the Agreement
entity ci: ConfidentialInfo

@ Optional copy that may be produced by the broker
entity copy: ConfidentialCopy

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Determine whether any of the Section‑3 exceptions apply to a piece of info
func exception_applies(info: ConfidentialInfo) -> bool:
    info.already_possessed
    or info.developed_independently
    or info.rightfully_received
    or info.inadvertent_disclosure
    or info.client_consent
    or info.service_disclosure
    or info.publicly_available

@ Determine whether the broker is legally compelled to disclose the info
func legally_compelled(info: ConfidentialInfo) -> bool:
    # The model does not capture the external legal trigger; it is a placeholder
    false   # (to be instantiated by an external fact when testing)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules – Deontic constraints (O = obligation, F = prohibition, P = permission)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Section 1.1 – Confidential information must be received in confidence
rule O receive_in_confidence:
    ci.owner = client
    implies (ci.received_by_brooks) (not exception_applies(ci)) always

@ Section 1.2 – Prohibit unauthorized disclosure
rule F disclose_without_consent:
    ci.received_by_brooks
    and not exception_applies(ci)
    implies (not (ci.owner = client and broker = broker)) always

@ Section 2.2 – Injunctive relief (modelled as a prohibition of breach)
rule F breach_causes_irreparable_harm:
    ci.received_by_brooks
    and not exception_applies(ci)
    implies (not (ci.owner = client and broker = broker)) always

@ Section 3 – Exceptions (modelled as permissions to copy/disclose)
rule P copy_allowed_under_exception:
    ci.received_by_brooks
    and exception_applies(ci)
    implies (exists copy: ConfidentialCopy . copy.original = ci and copy.created_by = broker) always

@ Section 4 – Compelled disclosure – must notify client first
rule O notify_before_compelled_disclosure:
    legally_compelled(ci)
    implies (ci.owner = client and broker = broker) always

@ Section 5 – Return or retention of material after termination
rule O return_or_retain_material:
    # The rule is abstracted: if client requests return, broker must return;
    # otherwise broker may retain for 3 years then dispose after 10 days.
    true always   # (implementation detail omitted – handled by external process)

@ Section 6 – Governing law (no operational effect, kept for traceability)
rule O governed_by_new_york_law:
    true always

@ Section 7 – Survival period (no operational effect, kept for traceability)
rule O agreement_survives_until_earliest_event:
    true always

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Hypothesis – “Receiving Party may create a copy of some Confidential Information in some circumstances.”
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ The hypothesis is captured by the permission rule above (copy_allowed_under_exception).
@ It is explicitly restated here for testing purposes.
rule P hypothesis_create_copy:
    ci.received_by_brooks
    and exception_applies(ci)
    implies (exists copy: ConfidentialCopy . copy.original = ci and copy.created_by = broker) always
```
